## The Magic Picture: A Lesson in Humility

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ଅଧ୍ୟାୟଟି ବୁଢୀରାମ ନାମକ ଜଣେ ଗର୍ବୀ ଲୋକ ଏବଂ କିଟୁ ନାମକ ଏକ ଚତୁର ପିଲା ବିଷୟରେ କହିଥାଏ। ବୁଢୀରାମ ତାଙ୍କ ଧନ ଏବଂ ଜିନିଷପତ୍ର ବିଷୟରେ ଗର୍ବ କରୁଥିଲେ।

This lesson introduces us to a character named Budhiram, who was very **proud** (ଗର୍ବୀ) of his **wealth** (ସମ୍ପତ୍ତି, ଧନ) and possessions. He owned unique items like a talking doll, a singing watch, and a magic pen. Budhiram would constantly talk about these things, which made the villagers **get bored** (ନିରସ ଲାଗିବା) and call him 'Buduram', implying he was foolish. This story teaches us about humility and not boasting.

### Kitu's Clever Trick

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ଶିକ୍ଷା ଦେବା ପାଇଁ ଏକ ଯୋଜନା କଲା। ସେ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ କିଛି ବିଶେଷ ଚିତ୍ରକଳା ଦେଖାଇବାକୁ ପ୍ରସ୍ତାବ ଦେଲା।

One day, a clever boy named Kitu decided to play a **trick** (କୌଶଳ) on Budhiram. Kitu told Budhiram that he had some special paintings from Delhi that were unlike any others. Budhiram, always eager to see and perhaps acquire unique things, wanted to see these paintings. The next day, he visited Kitu's house.

### The "Magic" Painting

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ପ୍ରଥମେ ବର୍ଷା ହେଉଥିବା ଏକ ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଲା ଯେଉଁଥିରେ ଛତା ଖୋଲା ଥିଲା। ପରେ, ସେହି ସମାନ ଦୃଶ୍ୟର ଅନ୍ୟ ଏକ ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଲା ଯେଉଁଥିରେ ଛତା ବନ୍ଦ ଥିଲା ଏବଂ ପାଗ ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟ ଥିଲା।

Kitu first showed Budhiram a painting of a girl crossing a bridge. It was raining in the picture, and the girl was holding an open umbrella. Kitu then suggested they wait for the

After a short break for tea, they returned to the room. To Budhiram's surprise, the painting now showed the same girl and bridge, but the umbrella was closed, and the sky was sunny and clear! Budhiram was amazed and asked how the umbrella had closed. Kitu simply replied that it had stopped raining, so the umbrella was closed.

### Budhiram's Misconception and Embarrassment

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ବୁଢୀରାମ ଭାବିଲେ ଯେ ଚିତ୍ରଟି ଯାଦୁକରୀ ଭାବରେ ବଦଳି ଯାଇଛି ଏବଂ ଏହାକୁ କିଣିବାକୁ ଚାହିଁଲେ। ସେ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀଙ୍କୁ ଏହାର ଯାଦୁ ଦେଖାଇବାକୁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କଲେ, କିନ୍ତୁ ବିଫଳ ହେଲେ।

Believing the painting was truly magical, Budhiram decided he must have it. He paid Kitu one thousand rupees, promising the rest later. Budhiram then called all the villagers to show off his new

## The Magic Picture: A Simple Story

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ଗପଟି ଜଣେ ଗର୍ବୀ ଲୋକ ବୁଢୀରାମ ଏବଂ କିଟୁ ନାମକ ଏକ ଚତୁର ପିଲା ବିଷୟରେ। ବୁଢୀରାମ ତାଙ୍କ ଜିନିଷ ବିଷୟରେ ବହୁତ ଗର୍ବ କରୁଥିଲେ।

This story is about a man named Budhiram. He was very **proud** (ଗର୍ବୀ) of everything he owned, like a special talking doll and a singing watch. He talked about his things so much that everyone in the village got tired of listening. They even started calling him 'Buduram' because he acted foolishly by boasting so much. [[2]], [[5]]

### Kitu's Smart Plan

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ଏକ ଶିକ୍ଷା ଦେବାକୁ ଚାହିଁଲା। ସେ କହିଲା ଯେ ତାଙ୍କ ପାଖରେ କିଛି ବିଶେଷ ଚିତ୍ରକଳା ଅଛି।

One day, a smart boy named Kitu wanted to teach Budhiram a lesson. Kitu told Budhiram that he had some amazing paintings that no one else had. Budhiram, who loved unique things, quickly went to Kitu's house to see them. [[2]]

### The Changing Picture

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ଦୁଇଟି ଭିନ୍ନ ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଲା, କିନ୍ତୁ ବୁଢୀରାମ ଭାବିଲେ ଯେ ଗୋଟିଏ ଚିତ୍ର ପାଗ ଅନୁସାରେ ବଦଳି ଯାଉଛି।

Kitu showed Budhiram a painting of a girl with an open umbrella because it was raining. Then, after they had some tea, Kitu showed him the *same* scene, but this time the girl's umbrella was closed, and the sun was shining! Budhiram was very surprised. He thought the painting had changed by itself, like magic. Kitu just said, "It stopped raining, so the umbrella closed." [[2]]

### Budhiram's Mistake

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ବୁଢୀରାମ ଭାବିଲେ ଏହା ଏକ ଯାଦୁକରୀ ଚିତ୍ର ଏବଂ ଏହାକୁ କିଣିଲେ। ସେ ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀଙ୍କୁ ଏହାର ଯାଦୁ ଦେଖାଇବାକୁ ଚେଷ୍ଟା କଲେ, କିନ୍ତୁ ବିଫଳ ହେଲେ।

Budhiram was so sure it was a magic painting that he bought it from Kitu for one thousand rupees. He then gathered all the villagers to show off his new "magic." He put water on the painting, expecting the umbrella to open, but nothing happened! The villagers laughed at him, and Budhiram felt very **ashamed** (ଲଜ୍ଜିତ). He **rushed to** (ତୁରନ୍ତ ବେଗରେ କରି ଚାଲିବା) Kitu's house, angry. [[2]]

### The Truth and the Lesson

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ବୁଝାଇଲା ଯେ ସେ ଦୁଇଟି ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଥିଲା, ଗୋଟିଏ ବର୍ଷା ପାଇଁ ଏବଂ ଅନ୍ୟଟି ଖରା ପାଇଁ। ବୁଢୀରାମ କେବଳ ଗୋଟିଏ କିଣିଥିଲେ। ଏହା ପରେ ବୁଢୀରାମ ଗର୍ବ କରିବା ବନ୍ଦ କରିଦେଲେ।

Kitu explained that he had two different paintings, one for rain and one for sunshine. Budhiram had only bought one of them. Budhiram finally understood he had been fooled. From that day, he stopped talking so much about his own things. The story teaches us that if you think you are too clever, someone else might be even cleverer! [[2]], [[3]]

**Example:**

**Question:** Why did Budhiram want to buy the painting?

**Answer:** Budhiram wanted to buy the painting because he believed it was a **magic** (ଯାଦୁକରୀ) painting that could change its scene with the weather. He was **proud** (ଗର୍ବୀ) and wanted to own something so unique and special to show off to others. [[2]]

## Helping Your Child Understand "The Magic Picture"

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ପାଠଟି ଆପଣଙ୍କ ପିଲାକୁ "The Magic Picture" ଗପ ବୁଝିବାରେ ସାହାଯ୍ୟ କରିବ। ଏହା ଗର୍ବୀ ବୁଢୀରାମ ଏବଂ ଚତୁର କିଟୁ ବିଷୟରେ କହିଥାଏ।

Hello! Let's read this interesting story, "The Magic Picture," together. It's about a man named Budhiram and a clever boy named Kitu. This story teaches us an important lesson about being humble.

### Meet Budhiram

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ବୁଢୀରାମ ଜଣେ ଗର୍ବୀ ଲୋକ ଥିଲେ ଯିଏ ତାଙ୍କ ଜିନିଷପତ୍ର ବିଷୟରେ ସବୁବେଳେ କହୁଥିଲେ। ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀମାନେ ତାଙ୍କୁ 'ବୁଦୁରାମ' ବୋଲି ଡାକୁଥିଲେ।

First, let's talk about Budhiram. The story says he was very **proud** (ଗର୍ବୀ) of his **wealth** (ସମ୍ପତ୍ତି, ଧନ) and all the special things he had, like a talking doll. He talked about them so much that the villagers got **bored** (ନିରସ ଲାଗିବା) and even called him 'Buduram'.

*Pause here and ask:* "What kind of person was Budhiram? Why did the villagers call him 'Buduram'?" (ପିଲାକୁ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ: "ବୁଢୀରାମ କିପରି ଲୋକ ଥିଲେ? ଗ୍ରାମବାସୀମାନେ ତାଙ୍କୁ 'ବୁଦୁରାମ' କାହିଁକି ଡାକୁଥିଲେ?") [[2]], [[5]]

### Kitu's Smart Idea

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ଏକ ଶିକ୍ଷା ଦେବା ପାଇଁ ଏକ ଯୋଜନା କଲା। ସେ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ କିଛି ବିଶେଷ ଚିତ୍ରକଳା ଦେଖାଇବାକୁ କହିଲା।

Now, a boy named Kitu decided to play a trick on Budhiram. He told Budhiram he had some very special paintings. Budhiram, being curious, went to Kitu's house to see them.

*Pause here and ask:* "Why do you think Kitu wanted to play a trick on Budhiram?" (ପିଲାକୁ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ: "ଆପଣ କାହିଁକି ଭାବୁଛନ୍ତି ଯେ କିଟୁ ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ଠକିବାକୁ ଚାହୁଁଥିଲା?") [[2]]

### The "Magic" Painting and the Truth

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: କିଟୁ ପ୍ରଥମେ ବର୍ଷା ହେଉଥିବା ଏକ ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଲା, ପରେ ଖରା ହେଉଥିବା ଅନ୍ୟ ଏକ ଚିତ୍ର ଦେଖାଇଲା। ବୁଢୀରାମ ଭାବିଲେ ଏହା ଏକ ଯାଦୁକରୀ ଚିତ୍ର।

Kitu showed Budhiram a painting of a girl with an open umbrella in the rain. After a tea break, he showed him another painting of the *same* girl, but this time the umbrella was closed, and the sun was out! Budhiram thought the painting had magically changed. He was so impressed that he bought one painting.

Later, Budhiram tried to show off his "magic" painting to the villagers, but it didn't work. Kitu then explained that he had two different paintings, not one magic one. Budhiram had only bought one. He felt very **ashamed** (ଲଜ୍ଜିତ).

*Pause here and ask:* "How did Kitu make Budhiram believe the painting was magic? What was the real truth?" (ପିଲାକୁ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ: "କିଟୁ କିପରି ବୁଢୀରାମଙ୍କୁ ବିଶ୍ୱାସ କରାଇଲା ଯେ ଚିତ୍ରଟି ଯାଦୁକରୀ ଥିଲା? ପ୍ରକୃତ ସତ୍ୟ କ'ଣ ଥିଲା?") [[2]], [[3]]

### The Lesson Learned

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ଘଟଣା ପରେ ବୁଢୀରାମ ଗର୍ବ କରିବା ବନ୍ଦ କରିଦେଲେ। ଗପଟି ଶିକ୍ଷା ଦିଏ ଯେ ଅତ୍ୟଧିକ ଗର୍ବ କରିବା ଭଲ ନୁହେଁ।

After this, Budhiram stopped boasting about his things. The story teaches us that if we think we are too clever or boast too much, we might end up looking foolish. It's better to be humble.

*Pause here and ask:* "What lesson did Budhiram learn from Kitu? What is the main message of this story?" (ପିଲାକୁ ପଚାରନ୍ତୁ: "ବୁଢୀରାମ କିଟୁଙ୍କ ଠାରୁ କେଉଁ ଶିକ୍ଷା ଗ୍ରହଣ କଲେ? ଏହି ଗପର ମୁଖ୍ୟ ବାର୍ତ୍ତା କ'ଣ?") [[3]]

questions: What kind of person was Budhiram? Why did the villagers call him 'Buduram'?
Why do you think Kitu wanted to play a trick on Budhiram?
How did Kitu make Budhiram believe the painting was magic? What was the real truth?
What happened when Budhiram tried to show the 'magic' to the villagers?
What lesson did Budhiram learn from Kitu? What is the main message of this story?
tips: Let your child think for a moment before answering. It's okay if they struggle a bit.
Encourage them to use their own words. Don't worry if their English isn't perfect; focus on understanding.
If they are stuck, gently guide them by re-reading a relevant part of the story or asking a simpler question.

variants.body_md:
## Exam Preparation: The Magic Picture

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ବିଭାଗଟି "The Magic Picture" ଗପରୁ ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ ଆସୁଥିବା ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ, ସାଧାରଣ ଭୁଲ ଏବଂ ଭଲ ନମ୍ବର ରଖିବା ପାଇଁ ଟିପ୍ସ ବିଷୟରେ।

This section provides key points for preparing for your BSE Odisha Class 8 English exam on "The Magic Picture" story. Focus on understanding the plot, characters, and moral.

### What gets asked

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ପରୀକ୍ଷାରେ ଚରିତ୍ର, ଘଟଣାକ୍ରମ, ଶବ୍ଦାର୍ଥ ଏବଂ ଗପର ନୈତିକ ଶିକ୍ଷା ଉପରେ ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ଆସିଥାଏ।

*   **Character Identification:** Questions about Budhiram's nature (e.g., proud, boastful) and Kitu's character (e.g., clever, tricky). Example: "What was Budhiram proud of?" [[2]], [[5]]
*   **Sequence of Events:** Questions requiring you to recall the order of events in the story. Example: "What did Kitu show Budhiram first, and what happened after tea?" [[2]]
*   **Vocabulary and Meaning:** Questions asking for the meaning of key words from the story or using them in sentences. Example: "What is the meaning of 'ashamed' (ଲଜ୍ଜିତ)?" [[3]]
*   **Plot Details:** Specific questions about the trick, the paintings, or Budhiram's actions. Example: "Why did Budhiram **rush to** (ତୁରନ୍ତ ବେଗରେ କରି ଚାଲିବା) Kitu's house after showing the painting to the villagers?" [[2]], [[3]]
*   **Moral of the Story:** Questions about the lesson learned from the story. Example: "What is the moral message of 'The Magic Picture'?" [[1]], [[3]]

### Common mistakes

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଛାତ୍ରମାନେ ପ୍ରାୟତଃ ଦୁଇଟି ଚିତ୍ରକୁ ଗୋଟିଏ ଭାବିଥାନ୍ତି, ଘଟଣାକ୍ରମ ଭୁଲ କରନ୍ତି, କିମ୍ବା ଗପର ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଶିକ୍ଷାକୁ ବୁଝିପାରନ୍ତି ନାହିଁ।

*   Confusing the two paintings as one magically changing picture.
*   Not clearly explaining Kitu's trick and the reason for Budhiram's embarrassment.
*   Misunderstanding why the villagers called Budhiram 'Buduram'.
*   Failing to connect Budhiram's pride to his downfall.
*   Giving incomplete answers, especially for questions about the moral.

### Scoring hints

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ସମ୍ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ବାକ୍ୟରେ ଉତ୍ତର ଦିଅନ୍ତୁ, ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଶବ୍ଦ ବ୍ୟବହାର କରନ୍ତୁ, ଘଟଣାକ୍ରମ ସଠିକ୍ ଭାବରେ ବର୍ଣ୍ଣନା କରନ୍ତୁ ଏବଂ ନୈତିକ ଶିକ୍ଷାକୁ ସ୍ପଷ୍ଟ ଭାବରେ ଲେଖନ୍ତୁ।

*   Always answer in complete sentences, showing full understanding.
*   Use key vocabulary words from the story (e.g., **proud**, **wealth**, **trick**, **ashamed**) correctly.
*   Clearly describe the sequence of events, especially how Kitu's trick unfolded.
*   When asked about the moral, state it clearly and explain how the story demonstrates it.
*   For character questions, provide specific examples from the text to support your answer.

### Practice prompts

ଓଡ଼ିଆ ସାରାଂଶ: ଏହି ପ୍ରଶ୍ନଗୁଡ଼ିକର ଅଭ୍ୟାସ କରନ୍ତୁ।

1.  Describe Budhiram's character at the beginning of the story. [[2]], [[5]]
2.  Explain how Kitu managed to fool Budhiram with the paintings. [[2]]
3.  What important lesson did Budhiram learn by the end of the story? [[3]]
